title: Structural Load Analysis
description: Perform a structural analysis of a simplified rocket body tube under launch loads. Model axial thrust loads, lateral wind loads, and vibration using finite element methods. Determine safety margins and identify critical failure points.
whyItMatters: Structural failure during launch is catastrophic and non-recoverable. Every rocket must be analysed for every load case it will experience — from transportation to the pad through max-Q to stage separation. This project teaches the analytical thinking that keeps rockets from breaking apart.

steps.title: Define the geometry
steps.description: Model a simplified cylindrical body tube (3.7m diameter, 40m length, aluminium-lithium alloy) representing a Falcon 9-class first stage. Include stiffening rings and tank dome.
steps.tips: Start with a 2D axisymmetric model before going to full 3D.

steps.title: Apply launch loads
steps.description: Calculate and apply: axial thrust load (9 x 845 kN), aerodynamic loads at max-Q (35 kPa dynamic pressure), and lateral wind loads (10 m/s wind at 10 km altitude).
steps.tips: Max-Q occurs at roughly 80 seconds into flight at about 12 km altitude.

steps.title: Run FEA
steps.description: Solve for stress distribution, deformation, and buckling modes using NASTRAN, Abaqus, or open-source FEA (CalculiX). Identify maximum stress locations.
steps.tips: Thin-walled cylinders under compression are buckling-critical. Check shell buckling first.

steps.title: Evaluate safety margins
steps.description: Calculate safety factors at critical locations. Aerospace standard is typically 1.25 yield and 1.4 ultimate for non-human-rated, 1.5 and 2.0 for human-rated.
steps.tips: If margins are negative, you need to thicken the skin, add stiffeners, or change materials.

steps.title: Optimise the structure
steps.description: Iterate on wall thickness and stiffener spacing to minimise mass while maintaining positive margins on all load cases. Document mass savings.
steps.tips: Every kilogram saved in structure is a kilogram of payload gained. Track the mass vs margin trade-off.

deliverables: Stress distribution plots for all load cases
Buckling mode shapes and critical loads
Optimised design with mass savings analysis

rubric.description: Appropriate mesh density, boundary conditions, and load application
All critical flight phases analysed with correct load magnitudes
Correct calculation with appropriate factors for aerospace application
Demonstrated mass reduction while maintaining structural integrity
